name: ask-user-interview
description: Create structured interactive interviews to collect user input, approvals, or clarifications before proceeding. Renders a markdown form in chat for the user to respond to. Use for approval gates, configuration choices, ambiguity resolution, or any decision requiring human input.

title.description: Interview title displayed to the user (e.g., 'Confirm deployment target')

purpose.description: Why this interview is needed — context shown to the user

questions.description: JSON array of question objects. Each: {id, type, prompt, required?, options?}. Types: text, single_choice, multi_choice, number, confirm

steps.template:
You need to collect input from the user.

Title: {{title}}
Purpose: {{purpose}}

Design the interview questions. Available question types:
- text: Free-form text (supports minLength, maxLength)
- single_choice: Pick one from options list
- multi_choice: Pick multiple from options list (supports minSelections, maxSelections)
- number: Numeric input (supports min, max, integerOnly)
- confirm: Yes/no boolean

Questions to use:
{{questions}}

Rules:
- Each question needs a unique kebab-case id
- Mark critical questions as required: true
- For choice questions, each option needs {id, label}
- Keep prompts concise and unambiguous
- Order questions logically (context first, decisions after)

Now render the interview as a markdown interactive form in chat.

Format the output as:

---
**{{title}}**
{{purpose}}

For each question, render based on type:
- text → labeled input field description
- single_choice → radio-style bullet list with options
- multi_choice → checkbox-style bullet list with options
- number → labeled numeric input with range hint
- confirm → Yes/No prompt

Example rendering:

**1. Choose deployment target** (required)
- ○ Staging
- ○ Production
- ○ Development

**2. Add release notes?**
- ◉ Yes
- ○ No

**3. Version number**
[number: 1-99, integers only]

---

After rendering, call ask_user_interview to create the interview and wait for responses.

The interview has been created. Poll for the user's response using action 'get' with the returned interviewId.

Once the user submits:
- Parse their responses
- Summarize the choices made
- Proceed with the next action based on their input

If the interview expires before response, inform the user and offer to recreate it.
